back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses after the death of two sons of Aaron, when they drew near before the face of the Lord and they died.

And the Lord said to Moses: Speak to Aaron your brother, that he should not enter at any time into the holy place behind the veil, in front of the mercy seat that is upon the ark, that he may not die; for in the cloud I will appear above the mercy seat.
With this Aaron shall enter the holy place: with a young bull, son of the herd, for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering.

A holy linen tunic he shall wear, and linen undergarments shall be on his flesh; and in a linen sash he shall gird, and in a linen turban he shall wrap—these are holy garments—and he shall bathe his flesh in water and put them on.
And from the congregation of the sons of Israel he shall take two male goats for a sin offering, and one ram for a burnt offering.
And Aaron shall present the bull of the sin offering that is for himself, and make atonement for himself and for his household.
And he shall take two goats and present them before the face of the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And Aaron shall cast lots upon the two goats—one lot for the Lord and one lot for Azazel.
And Aaron shall present the goat upon which the lot came for the Lord, and make it a sin offering.
And the goat upon which the lot came for Azazel shall stand alive before the face of the Lord, to make atonement on it, to send it to Azazel into the wilderness.
And Aaron shall present the bull of the sin offering that is for himself, and make atonement for himself and for his household, and he shall kill the bull of the sin offering that is for himself.
And he shall take a full censer of burning coals from the altar before the face of the Lord, and his two handfuls of finely ground fragrant incense, and bring them inside the veil.
And he shall put the incense on the fire before the face of the Lord, and the cloud of incense shall cover the mercy seat that is above the testimony, that he may not die.

And he shall take some of the bull's blood and sprinkle it with his finger on the face of the mercy seat toward the east, and before the mercy seat he shall sprinkle seven times from the blood with his finger.

And he shall kill the goat of the sin offering that is for the people, and bring its blood inside the veil, and do with its blood as he did with the bull's blood—and sprinkle it on the mercy seat and before the mercy seat.

And he shall make atonement for the holy place from the uncleannesses of the sons of Israel and from their rebellious acts, for all their sins; and so he shall do for the tent of meeting that abides among them in the midst of their uncleannesses.

And no man shall be in the tent of meeting when he goes in to make atonement in the holy place until he comes out; and he shall make atonement for himself and for his household and for all the assembly of Israel.

And he shall go out to the altar that is before the face of the Lord and make atonement upon it, and take some of the bull's blood and some of the goat's blood, and put it on the horns of the altar all around.
And he shall sprinkle upon it some of the blood with his finger seven times, and cleanse it and consecrate it from the uncleannesses of the sons of Israel.
And he shall finish making atonement for the holy place and the tent of meeting and the altar, and shall present the live goat.

And Aaron shall lay both his hands on the head of the live goat, and confess upon it all the iniquities of the sons of Israel and all their rebellious acts, for all their sins; and he shall put them on the head of the goat and send it by the hand of an appointed man into the wilderness.
And the goat shall carry upon itself all their iniquities to a solitary land, and he shall release the goat in the wilderness.
And Aaron shall enter the tent of meeting, and take off the linen garments that he wore when entering the holy place, and leave them there.

And he shall bathe his flesh with water in a holy place, and put on his garments, and go out and offer his burnt offering and the burnt offering of the people, and make atonement for himself and for the people.
And the fat of the sin offering he shall burn as smoke on the altar.
And the one who sends the goat to Azazel shall wash his clothes and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he may enter the camp.

And the bull of the sin offering and the goat of the sin offering, whose blood was brought to make atonement in the holy place, shall be taken outside the camp, and they shall burn in fire their hides and their flesh and their dung.
And the one who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he may enter the camp.

And this shall be for you a permanent statute: in the seventh month, on the tenth of the month, you shall humble your souls, and you shall do no work—the native and the foreigner who resides in your midst.
For on this day atonement shall be made for you, to cleanse you from all your sins; before the face of the Lord you shall be clean.
It is a Sabbath of Sabbaths to you, and you shall humble your souls; it is a permanent statute.
And the priest who shall be anointed and who shall be ordained to serve as priest in the place of his father shall make atonement, and he shall put on the linen garments, the holy garments.
And he shall make atonement for the most holy place, and the tent of meeting, and the altar he shall atone for, and upon the priests and upon all the people of the assembly he shall make atonement.
And this shall be for you a permanent statute, to make atonement for the sons of Israel from all their sins once in the year. And he did as the Lord commanded Moses.
